---
This PR introduces the AgentIdentity graph node schema and a seed script to provision persistent identities in the Neo.mjs memory core graph.

### Architectural Impact
* Resolves #10144
* Adopts **per-model** identity binding (e.g. `@neo-opus-ada`, `@neo-gemini-pro`) rather than ephemeral session-based or monolithic agent accounts. This ensures stability of accountability and traceability while supporting graph traversal aligned with GitHub author logs.
* Idempotent seeding mechanism correctly manages `createdAt` timestamps for pre-existing graph nodes vs. new ones.

### Files Modified
* `ai/scripts/seedAgentIdentities.mjs`: Idempotent provisioning script.
* `learn/agentos/IdentitySchema.md`: Formal documentation on schema parameters and the per-model binding rationale.

### Edge Cases Handled
* Properly checks for the existence of previous raw SQLite nodes to conditionally retain `createdAt` timestamps before performing standard `Memory_GraphService.upsertNode()` operations.

## Comments

### `@tobiu` commented on 2026-04-21T14:54:13Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Request Changes
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Solid scope execution — the per-model binding decision is correctly captured in `IdentitySchema.md`, the idempotent seeder handles the `createdAt`-preservation edge case defensively, and the schema table matches #10144's specification. One blocker in the documentation (MX is **Model Experience**, not Agent Experience — a category mistake that would propagate if merged) plus a couple of polish items before greenlight. Also flagging a cross-PR dependency with #10160 for merge ordering.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 85 - 15 points deducted because `learn/agentos/IdentitySchema.md` says *"To support the 'Agent Experience' (MX) capabilities"* — but MX is **Model Experience**, not Agent Experience. AX = Agent Experience (Biilmann/Netlify, pre-Neo). MX is the Neo-coined term for substrate-evolving-via-model-friction (Discussion #10137). Conflating them propagates a category mistake the project explicitly disambiguated.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 80 - 20 points deducted: (a) PR body doesn't address testing strategy — the idempotent `createdAt`-preservation logic has no unit test protecting it from regression; (b) no trade-off discussion of hardcoded-JS-identities vs. JSONL-data-file approach (cf. `.neo-ai-data/concepts/*.jsonl` precedent); (c) the defensive SQLite peek in `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` lacks a comment explaining why `createdAt` is conditionally stripped before upsert — non-obvious, violates the Anchor & Echo mandate.
> *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 90 - 10 points deducted: (a) no unit test coverage for the idempotent-retention logic; (b) `createdAt: new Date().toISOString()` evaluates at module-load time rather than at insertion time — fragile if the module is ever imported rather than run as main.
> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 - AgentIdentity schema + seed mechanism ship; unblocks #10145 (OAuth2) and downstream identity work.
> *   **`[IMPACT]`**: 95 - 5 points deducted because schema-only until OAuth2 (#10145) wires in runtime verification; empirical value is structural-layer-only until a downstream consumer ships.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 30 - Low: schema table + seed script + doc; no new runtime primitives or algorithmic complexity.
> *   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Quick Win - High ROI for scaffolding that unblocks identity-layer work.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10144
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** AgentIdentity, agentos.mx, agentos.identity, oauth2, #10016
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: `IdentitySchema.md` §Architecture & Rationale conflates MX (Model Experience) with AX (Agent Experience). The distinction was explicitly drawn in Discussion #10137. Post-merge, `IdentitySchema.md` becomes the documentation anchor for future agents; a category mistake here would propagate across future reads.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: N/A
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Per-model-vs-per-version decision landed with clean rationale (low churn, cross-session traversal, traceability). Worth preserving as the reference decision for future identity-schema questions.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge**: Beyond the MX/AX conflation (addressed in Required Actions below), three non-blocking concerns worth surfacing:
> 
> 1. **Hardcoded identity list.** When we add `@neo-haiku-4-5`, `@neo-sonnet-4-6`, or any future model identity, we edit this script. The `concepts/*.jsonl` pattern (data-file over hardcoded constants) would scale better and keep git-diff-reviewable provisioning. For v1 with 3 identities this is fine; worth flagging as a follow-up if the list grows.
> 2. **`modelFamily: null` for the human identity.** Property is present but null for `@tobiu`. Could omit entirely — `accountType === 'human'` is the discriminator; `modelFamily: null` is redundant shape.
> 3. **Defensive `createdAt` retention reads raw SQLite.** The workaround peeks directly at `Memory_GraphService.db.storage.db.prepare(...)` to check if the existing node has `createdAt`. If `upsertNode`'s semantics ever change to preserve-existing-if-not-in-update, this extra code becomes cruft. A comment explaining the defensive pattern would help future maintainers.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔗 Cross-Skill Integration Audit
> 
> Per guide §8.1, this PR introduces a new architectural primitive (AgentIdentity node type) and a new documentation file. Audit:
> 
> - [x] Does any existing skill document a predecessor step that should now fire this new pattern? — AgentIdentity is a new primitive consumed by future work (#10145 OAuth2, #10146 cross-tenant perms, mailbox #10139); no existing skill is a predecessor.
> - [ ] Does `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` §9 need updating? — No, AgentIdentity isn't a skill. But `IdentitySchema.md` is a new `learn/agentos/` doc — a future `CodebaseOverview.md` rewrite (#10136) should reference it. Not blocking here; track when #10136 lands.
> - [ ] Does any reference file mention a predecessor pattern that should also mention the new one? — `IdentitySchema.md` "Ingestion Mechanism" section could reference the sibling-pattern services (`ConceptIngestor`, `IssueIngestor`, `FileSystemIngestor`) for maintenance-context readers. Minor polish.
> - N/A new MCP tool
> - [x] New convention (per-model identity) documented.
> 
> **Findings:** MX/AX category mistake (see Required Actions). Optional polish on ingestion-mechanism cross-links. No latent integration gap.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### ⚠️ Cross-PR Dependency
> 
> This PR's diff includes the `ticket-intake-workflow.md` changes from #10160. The branch was cut off #10160 rather than dev, so the ticket-intake diff appears in both PRs. Recommended merge order:
> 
> 1. Merge #10160 first (smaller scope, faster turnaround after its Required Actions).
> 2. Rebase #10162 onto dev to drop the duplicate ticket-intake commit.
> 3. Merge #10162.
> 
> Flagging so we don't accidentally merge #10162 first and create a conflict for #10160.
> 
> ---
>

Discussion #10137 for the distinction from Agent Experience (AX)."* Prevents the category mistake from propagating.
> - [ ] Add a comment in `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` explaining the defensive `createdAt` retention logic (why the raw SQLite peek; what happens if `upsertNode` semantics change). Anchor & Echo the "idempotent upsert without clobbering creation-time provenance" intent.
> - [ ] After #10160 merges, rebase this branch onto dev to drop the duplicate ticket-intake commit.
> 
> Addressed, and I'll gladly re-review and greenlight.
